affordances.name: Temporary support with fade plan
affordances.mechanism: When complexity exceeds current capacity, break the task into sequenced support, modeling, guided practice, correction, and handoff so the learner reaches independent performance rather than permanent dependence on the framework.

use_when: The learner or team cannot yet hold the whole system in mind but can master the next increment.
Order, pacing, and support determine whether people build competence or confusion.
Temporary structure can later be removed as judgment and autonomy develop.
do_not_use_when: The scaffold becomes permanent bureaucracy rather than a path to autonomy.
The framework is the wrong fit for the problem assumptions.
The group is using structure to delay action after enough learning has occurred.
case_evidence_needed: The modeling, guided practice, correction, and handoff sequence.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to define the staged support, the learner handoff, and the condition under which the scaffold should be reduced or removed.
evidence_required: The current complexity that exceeds capacity.
good_output_shape: Chunks the task into a next manageable step.
Names when the scaffold comes down.

diagnostic_questions: What is the smallest next chunk the learner can manage?
What support is temporary, and how will it be faded?
Is the scaffold solving overload or creating dependency?
misuse_guards: Do not make the scaffold permanent.
Do not let the first framework become immune to disconfirming evidence.
Do not simplify so much that the learner cannot transfer judgment.

source_evidence.source_quote: temporary support structures are erected around a building
Most useful when complexity exceeds current capacity
Danger When the Scaffold Never Comes Down

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 12 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports scaffolding as staged temporary support that should transfer control.
dropped_material: Did not extract AI persona scaffolding as a runtime prompt affordance.
Kept consulting-framework examples as source context only.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether this card creates useful support design without permanent process overhead.

absence_records.reason: The source defines scaffolding as temporary support toward mastery; permanent dependence is explicitly a danger.

source_evidence.source_quote: Best when temporary structure can later be removed

absence_records.reason: The source says the goal is staged movement toward independent performance; support without a removal condition is not reviewed depth.

source_evidence.source_quote: gradual hand-over of control and mastery

absence_records.reason: Generic scaffolding owns broad staged support and workflow sequencing. When the case specifically turns on novice instructional degree-of-freedom reduction, route to scaffolding-educational so the receiver preserves the instructional mastery and fading contract.

source_evidence.source_quote: Best when temporary structure can later be removed
temporary support structured for gradual mastery
simplifying complexity to facilitate mastery and learning

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 12 source reading found strong reviewed depth for scaffolding as temporary support and transfer of responsibility.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet review should check whether this card improves support sequencing without adding bureaucracy.
